task_completion.description: A new Markups Point List named 'Navigation_Landmarks' is created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Markups module panel (usually on the left), the list selector dropdown or subject hierarchy shows the active list name as 'Navigation_Landmarks'.

task_completion.description: Fiducial markers are placed at the Nasion (nose bridge) and Inion (back of skull).
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Green (Sagittal) slice view, a point is visible at the indentation of the nose bridge (front) and another at the occipital protuberance (back of head).

task_completion.description: Fiducial markers are placed at the Left and Right Tragus (ear canal openings).
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Red (Axial) slice view or 3D view, points are visible on the left and right sides of the head, aligned with the ear canals.

task_completion.description: The four fiducial points are explicitly renamed to Nasion, Inion, Left_Tragus, and Right_Tragus.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Markups module control panel displays a table where the point labels read exactly 'Nasion', 'Inion', 'Left_Tragus', and 'Right_Tragus'.

task_completion.description: The markup list is saved to the correct path as a JSON file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save' dialog, recent files list, or file browser window shows 'navigation_landmarks.mrk.json' saved in '~/Documents/SlicerData/BraTS/'.

task_completion.description: A screenshot of the placed landmarks is captured to the specified file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file browser shows 'landmarks_screenshot.png' exists, or the agent is observed using the screen capture tool to save this file.

integrity.description: The agent uses the 3D Slicer GUI (mouse clicks on slices) to place points.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the mouse cursor interacting with the Red/Green/Yellow slice views or 3D view to drop points, rather than typing coordinates into a terminal or python console.

integrity.description: The output file is generated via the software's save function, not manually typed.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent does not open a text editor (like Notepad or vim) to write the .mrk.json file content manually.

integrity.description: The task is performed within the actual 3D Slicer application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The application window contains standard Slicer elements: the module panel on the left, and the 2x2 layout of Red/Yellow/Green/3D views.
